message:
style: rustfmt pass across frankenterm-core, mux, escape-parser, and fuzz targets

Mechanical rustfmt output with no semantic changes. 26 files touched
across four crates. Applied so the tree matches the formatter a
sibling agent had already run locally; committing the delta avoids
per-file re-formatting churn during future edits.

Families of changes (all driven by `cargo fmt` defaults, no rustfmt.toml):

- Import ordering: reordering items within `use std::sync::{...}` blocks
  so `Arc` sorts after `atomic::{...}` (alphabetical within module
  path), and re-wrapping of split `use` trees. Hit across
  `frankenterm/mux/src/{client,connui,domain,events,lib,localpane,pane,
  ssh,ssh_agent,tab,termwiztermtab,tmux,tmux_commands,tmux_pty}.rs`
  and `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs`.

- Line-wrap preference swaps: moving `let binding: Type = expr
  .method().method();` fragments between single-line and
  chained-call-per-line styles. Visible in
  `crates/frankenterm-core/src/{events,mcp_client,storage}.rs`,
  `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/{mcp_conformance_core_tools,
  metamorphic_pattern_engine,wa_events_mcp_conformance}.rs`,
  `frankenterm/escape-parser/{src/tmux_cc/mod.rs,tests/proptest_parse_first.rs}`,
  and the three fuzz targets
  (`fuzz/fuzz_targets/{escape_parser_raw,ftvi_index_from_bytes,
  native_events_wire}.rs`).

- `assert_eq!` / `prop_assert_eq!` argument splitting: multi-line
  argument lists that were previously collapsed onto one line are
  split into one argument per line by the formatter's 100-column
  budget for macro calls (e.g. in
  `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs`'s repeated
  `assert_eq!(data.get("query"), Some(&Value::String(...)))` blocks).

- `prop_map` closure body re-wrapping where the formatter lifted the
  returned struct literal onto its own braced block (e.g.
  `frankenterm/escape-parser/src/tmux_cc/mod.rs` `ScriptBlock::Guarded {..}`
  strategy).

No tests, APIs, features, dependency graph entries, or feature flags
change. `git diff --stat` reports 26 files / ~159 ins / ~129 del, all
whitespace and reordering.
